description_long: This is a rustic wooden fence composed of two thick vertical posts connected by two horizontal rails and two diagonal cross braces, forming an 'X' pattern between the posts. The wood appears to be dark-stained, with a natural, slightly irregular texture that mimics hand-hewn timber. The fence is relatively low in height, suitable for marking boundaries in parks, gardens, or along urban walkways. The top edge of the fence is mostly flat, with the posts extending slightly above the rails. There are no visible decorative elements or post caps, giving it a simple, utilitarian appearance. The construction suggests it is sturdy enough to act as a barrier but not intended to prevent determined passage.
description: A dark-stained wooden fence with two posts, two horizontal rails, and diagonal cross braces forming an 'X' pattern. The design is rustic and simple, with a flat top and no decorative features.
description_view_0: A narrow end-on view showing the thickness of one of the vertical posts and the edge of the rails.
description_view_1: A full frontal view displaying the 'X' cross-brace pattern, two horizontal rails, and both vertical posts.
description_view_2: Another narrow end-on view, similar to view 0, showing the opposite post and rail edge.
description_view_3: A rear view nearly identical to the front, showing the same 'X' cross-brace and rail arrangement.
